name: Colleges & Culture Plus
tagline: Yale → Boston → Stowe skiing → Montréal → Tremblant day trip → Hadley cottage → home
vibe: Full culture immersion with 2 ski days, a Tremblant day trip, and a relaxed drive home via the Adirondacks. No 8-hour marathon drives.

days.label: Day 1 — Saturday, Apr 11

legs.notes: I-95 N. Charge at New Haven Supercharger during Yale visit.
I-95 N through Providence. Boston traffic adds time in afternoon.

segments.activity: Depart Hopewell — early start
Yale campus walk — Old Campus, Harkness Tower, Beinecke Library
segments.notes: Charge the car while you explore — New Haven Supercharger is nearby.

segments.activity: Lunch on Wooster Street — Frank Pepe's or Sally's Apizza
Depart New Haven for Boston
Arrive Cambridge — check in to hotel
Evening walk — Boston Common and Public Garden
Dinner at Faneuil Hall Marketplace — explore the food stalls

days.notes: No Yale overnight — you get the campus experience without the extra night. Supercharger ~$15.

segments.activity: Breakfast at Sofra Bakery
Harvard Admissions tour (pre-register)
Lunch in Harvard Square
MIT self-guided tour — Infinite Corridor, Stata Center, Killian Court
Walk the Charles River Esplanade at sunset
Dinner in Roxbury — explore the neighborhood

days.label: Day 3 — Monday, Apr 13

segments.activity: Museum of African American History + African Meeting House
Freedom Trail walking tour — start at Boston Common
segments.notes: 2.5 miles. Wear comfortable shoes. Free self-guided.

segments.activity: Old North Church — 'One if by land, two if by sea'
Boston Waterfront — Seaport District stroll
Coffee and snack at Dudley Café, Roxbury
Dinner near Faneuil Hall / North End

days.notes: Full Boston day — history, Black culture, and waterfront all in one.

days.label: Day 4 — Tuesday, Apr 14

legs.notes: I-93 N → I-89 N → VT-100 N to Stowe. Quick charge in Montpelier on the way.

segments.activity: Depart Cambridge — early start for ski day
Charge + coffee in Montpelier
Arrive Stowe — pick up ski rentals
segments.notes: Reserve rentals in advance at stowe.com. ~$65/person for full package.

segments.activity: Ski! Beginner lessons + open runs on Mt. Mansfield
segments.notes: Lift tickets ~$150/person spring rate. Book multi-pack for savings. Beginner lessons extra ~$100/person if needed.

segments.activity: Après-ski — hot chocolate, browse Stowe village
Dinner in Stowe Village
Check in to lodge — charge car overnight

days.notes: Skiing earlier in the trip! Stowe is Vermont's premier resort and conveniently on the route to Montréal.

days.label: Day 5 — Wednesday, Apr 15

legs.notes: VT-100 S → I-89 N → border crossing into Québec. Passport required. Car should be full from overnight L2 charging at the lodge.

segments.activity: Breakfast at the lodge, depart Stowe
Depart for Montréal
Arrive Montréal — check in, first look at Old Montréal
Notre-Dame Basilica — stunning Gothic interior
segments.notes: Book tickets in advance at basiliquenotredame.ca. ~$8/person.

segments.activity: Explore Place Jacques-Cartier + Old Port
Dinner — Haitian cuisine in Little Burgundy

days.notes: Bring passports. US-Canada border crossing on I-89 is typically smooth. Short drive leaves a full afternoon in Montréal.

segments.activity: Morning walk through Little Burgundy — Oscar Peterson's neighborhood
Jean-Talon Market — the largest open-air market in North America
segments.notes: Budget for maple products, cheeses, and spices to bring home.

segments.activity: Lunch at the market
McGill University campus walk
Mount Royal Park — Kondiaronk Belvedere city viewpoint
Old Montréal evening — cobblestone streets, boutiques
Dinner in Little Burgundy — Caribbean or African diaspora cuisine

days.label: Day 7 — Friday, Apr 17

legs.notes: Autoroute 15 N → 117. Charge car in Montréal before departing.
Same route back. Return to your same Montréal accommodation.

segments.activity: Early departure for Tremblant
Arrive Tremblant — pick up ski rentals
segments.notes: Reserve rentals in advance at tremblant.ca. ~$65/person.

segments.activity: Ski at Tremblant! Beginner-friendly runs + village views
segments.notes: Lift tickets ~$140/person spring rate. Tremblant has excellent beginner terrain.

segments.activity: Late lunch on the mountain
Browse the pedestrian village at the base
Depart Tremblant — drive back to Montréal
Arrive back in Montréal — relax at hotel
Farewell dinner in Old Montréal

days.notes: Day trip to Tremblant — no need to pack up and change hotels. Drive back to the same Montréal accommodation and enjoy a farewell dinner.

days.label: Day 8 — Saturday, Apr 18

legs.notes: Autoroute 15 S → I-87 S (Adirondack Northway). Beautiful drive through the Adirondacks. Charge in Plattsburgh on the way.

segments.activity: Breakfast + pack up, depart Montréal
Depart for Hadley — cross back into the US
Charge + stretch in Plattsburgh
Arrive at the cottage in Hadley, NY
Lunch and relax — Great Sacandaga Lake views
segments.notes: Pick up groceries on the way in, or eat at a local spot.

segments.activity: Afternoon at the cottage — unwind, walk by the lake
Dinner at the cottage or nearby in Hadley/Lake Luzerne

days.notes: Break up the drive home with a night at the cottage. No hotel cost — just a relaxing transition day through the southern Adirondacks.

days.label: Day 9 — Sunday, Apr 19

legs.notes: I-87 S → I-287 E → I-78 W into NJ. Easy Sunday drive — arrive by mid-afternoon.

segments.activity: Breakfast at the cottage, close up
Depart Hadley for home
Charge + coffee near Albany
Arrive home — trip complete!

days.notes: Easy 3.5-hour drive. No marathon haul — you broke it up with the cottage stop.
